heading: Examples
body:
The following examples use the `movies` collection in the
`sample_mflix` database with a custom index definition that uses the
[keyword analyzer](ref-keyword-analyzer). If you have the
[sample data sets](available-sample-datasets) on your cluster, you
can create a MongoDB Search index on the `movies` collection and run the
example queries on your cluster.**Atlas UI:**Atlas UITo view and edit query syntax in the Search Tester:* Click the Query button to the right of the index
  to query.

* Click Edit Query to view a default query
  syntax sample in JSON (Javascript Object Notation) format.To learn how to run the following queries in the Atlas UI, see
[Search Tester](atlas-search-queries).**Shell:**MongoDB ShellRun the following command at [mongosh](mongodb-shell) prompt to use the
`sample_mflix` database:```shell
use sample_mflix
```To learn how to run the following queries in the
[mongosh](mongodb-shell), see [Search Tester](atlas-search-queries).**Compass:**CompassTo learn how to run the following queries in the MongoDB Compass, see
[Search Tester](atlas-search-queries).**C#:**C#To learn how to run the following queries by using the
[MongoDB C# driver](csharp), see
[Search Tester](atlas-search-queries).**Go:**GoTo learn how to run the following queries by using the
[MongoDB Go# driver](go), see
[Search Tester](atlas-search-queries).**Java-sync:**Java (Sync)To learn how to run the following queries by using the
[MongoDB Java driver](java-drivers), see
[Search Tester](atlas-search-queries).**Kotlin-coroutine:**Kotlin (Coroutine)To learn how to run the following queries by using the
[MongoDB Kotlin driver](kotlin-drivers), see
[Search Tester](atlas-search-queries).**Nodejs:**Node.jsTo learn how to run the following queries by using the
[MongoDB Node Driver](node), see
[Search Tester](atlas-search-queries).**Python:**PythonTo learn how to run the following queries by using the
[PyMongo driver](pymongo), see
[Search Tester](atlas-search-queries).
